texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1 Introduction
texts.text: 1 Introduction

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patch generates an email message through the VistA MailMan application when a new causative agent is added to a Patient Allergies File (#120.8) and the VA Drug Class is not specified in the VA Drug Class field (#3).
texts.text: This patch generates an email message through the VistA MailMan application when a new causative agent is added to a Patient Allergies File (#120.8) and the VA Drug Class is not specified in the VA Drug Class field (#3).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This modification to VistA originated as a field-developed enhancement for local use at a VA facility. It was determined that the modification would be of benefit to the wider VistA user community, and so it is now being deployed nationally.
texts.text: This modification to VistA originated as a field-developed enhancement for local use at a VA facility. It was determined that the modification would be of benefit to the wider VistA user community, and so it is now being deployed nationally.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following New Service Request (NSR) is resolved with this patch:
texts.text: The following New Service Request (NSR) is resolved with this patch:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: NSR20140712  – Send Mail Message When VA Drug Class Field is Empty
texts.text: NSR20140712  – Send Mail Message When VA Drug Class Field is Empty

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2 Purpose
texts.text: 2 Purpose

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The purpose of this document is to communicate changes to the VistA system resulting from implementation of GMRA*4.0*50.
texts.text: The purpose of this document is to communicate changes to the VistA system resulting from implementation of GMRA*4.0*50.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3 Audience
texts.text: 3 Audience

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This document targets users of Adverse Reaction Tracking in VistA and the Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS).
texts.text: This document targets users of Adverse Reaction Tracking in VistA and the Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS).

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4 This Release
texts.text: 4 This Release

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following sections provide a summary of the new features and enhancements introduced by this patch, as well as any known issues.
texts.text: The following sections provide a summary of the new features and enhancements introduced by this patch, as well as any known issues.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4.1 New Features and Functions Added
texts.text: 4.1 New Features and Functions Added

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patch adds no new VistA or CPRS features or functions.
texts.text: This patch adds no new VistA or CPRS features or functions.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4.2 Enhancements and Modifications to Existing
texts.text: 4.2 Enhancements and Modifications to Existing

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Patch GMRA*4*50 generates an email message through the VistA MailMan application when a new causative agent is added to a Patient Allergies File (#120.8) and the VA Drug Class is not specified in the VA Drug Class field (#3). Causative agents are medications and substances to which the patient exhibits an adverse reaction or allergy, and are tracked for each patient in the Patient Allergies File (#120.8) using the VistA Adverse Reaction Tracking package. The email message is sent the first time a new causative agent without a designated VA Drug Class is added for a patient. Subsequent observations associated with the same causative agent do not trigger an email. The email is sent only once for each causative agent without a designated VA Drug Class added to a Patient Allergies File (#120.8).
texts.text: Patch GMRA*4*50 generates an email message through the VistA MailMan application when a new causative agent is added to a Patient Allergies File (#120.8) and the VA Drug Class is not specified in the VA Drug Class field (#3). Causative agents are medications and substances to which the patient exhibits an adverse reaction or allergy, and are tracked for each patient in the Patient Allergies File (#120.8) using the VistA Adverse Reaction Tracking package. The email message is sent the first time a new causative agent without a designated VA Drug Class is added for a patient. Subsequent observations associated with the same causative agent do not trigger an email. The email is sent only once for each causative agent without a designated VA Drug Class added to a Patient Allergies File (#120.8).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The email message is directed to members of a predefined email group. This email group, “ADVERSE_ALLERGY_WARNING,” is created by a post-installation routine if it is not present upon installation of the patch. The email group must be populated with the email addresses of individuals to be notified when a new causative agent is added to a Patient Allergies File (#120.8) without a designated VA Drug Class in the VA Drug Class field (#3).
texts.text: The email message is directed to members of a predefined email group. This email group, “ADVERSE_ALLERGY_WARNING,” is created by a post-installation routine if it is not present upon installation of the patch. The email group must be populated with the email addresses of individuals to be notified when a new causative agent is added to a Patient Allergies File (#120.8) without a designated VA Drug Class in the VA Drug Class field (#3).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following new routine was added to implement the enhancement introduced by this patch:
texts.text: The following new routine was added to implement the enhancement introduced by this patch:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: GMRA50P1 – This routine builds the ADVERSE_ALLERGY_WARNING email group, if it does not already exist. Individuals who should receive an email indicating a causative agent was added to a patient file without a defined VA Drug Class must be added to this group.
texts.text: GMRA50P1 – This routine builds the ADVERSE_ALLERGY_WARNING email group, if it does not already exist. Individuals who should receive an email indicating a causative agent was added to a patient file without a defined VA Drug Class must be added to this group.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following routines were modified to implement the enhancement introduced by this patch:
texts.text: The following routines were modified to implement the enhancement introduced by this patch:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: GMRAPEM0 – This routine creates a subroutine that determines if the causative agent passed to it has a defined VA Drug Class. If not, an email is sent to the ADVERSE_ALLERY_WARNING email group notifying members of the undefined VA Drug Class.
texts.text: GMRAPEM0 – This routine creates a subroutine that determines if the causative agent passed to it has a defined VA Drug Class. If not, an email is sent to the ADVERSE_ALLERY_WARNING email group notifying members of the undefined VA Drug Class.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This routine was also modified to pass the identity of the new causative agent to this new subroutine for processing for the patient whose Patient Allergies File (#120.8) is being edited in VistA.
texts.text: This routine was also modified to pass the identity of the new causative agent to this new subroutine for processing for the patient whose Patient Allergies File (#120.8) is being edited in VistA.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: GMRAGUI1 – This routine was modified to pass the identity of the new causative agent to the new subroutine in GMRAPEM0 for processing for the patient whose Patient Allergies File (#120.8) is being edited in CPRS.
texts.text: GMRAGUI1 – This routine was modified to pass the identity of the new causative agent to the new subroutine in GMRAPEM0 for processing for the patient whose Patient Allergies File (#120.8) is being edited in CPRS.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patch may be installed with users on the system although it is  recommended that it be installed during non-peak hours to minimize  potential disruption to users.  This patch should take less than 5  minutes to install.
texts.text: This patch may be installed with users on the system although it is  recommended that it be installed during non-peak hours to minimize  potential disruption to users.  This patch should take less than 5  minutes to install.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 6.2 Installation Instructions
texts.text: 6.2 Installation Instructions

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 1.	Choose the PackMan message containing this patch.
texts.text: 1.	Choose the PackMan message containing this patch.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 2.	Choose the INSTALL/CHECK MESSAGE PackMan option.
texts.text: 2.	Choose the INSTALL/CHECK MESSAGE PackMan option.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 3.	From the Kernel Installation and Distribution System Menu, select the Installation Menu.  From this menu, you may elect to use the following options. When prompted for the INSTALL NAME enter GMRA*4.0*50.
texts.text: 3.	From the Kernel Installation and Distribution System Menu, select the Installation Menu.  From this menu, you may elect to use the following options. When prompted for the INSTALL NAME enter GMRA*4.0*50.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: a. Verify Checksums in Transport Global - This option will allow you to ensure the integrity of the routines that are in the transport global.
texts.text: a. Verify Checksums in Transport Global - This option will allow you to ensure the integrity of the routines that are in the transport global.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: b.  Print Transport Global - This option will allow you to view the components of the KIDS build.
texts.text: b.  Print Transport Global - This option will allow you to view the components of the KIDS build.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: c. Compare Transport Global to Current System - This option will allow you to view all changes that will be made when this patch is installed.  It compares all components of this patch (routines, DD's, templates, etc.).
texts.text: c. Compare Transport Global to Current System - This option will allow you to view all changes that will be made when this patch is installed.  It compares all components of this patch (routines, DD's, templates, etc.).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: d.  Backup a Transport Global - This option will create a backup message of any routines exported with this patch. It will not backup any other changes such as DD's or templates.
texts.text: d.  Backup a Transport Global - This option will create a backup message of any routines exported with this patch. It will not backup any other changes such as DD's or templates.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 4. 	From the Installation Menu, select the Install Package(s) option and choose the patch to install.
texts.text: 4. 	From the Installation Menu, select the Install Package(s) option and choose the patch to install.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 5.	If prompted 'Want KIDS to Rebuild Menu Trees Upon Completion of Install? NO//' press <Enter>.
texts.text: 5.	If prompted 'Want KIDS to Rebuild Menu Trees Upon Completion of Install? NO//' press <Enter>.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 6.	When prompted 'Want KIDS to INHIBIT LOGONs during the install? NO// Press <Enter>
texts.text: 6.	When prompted 'Want KIDS to INHIBIT LOGONs during the install? NO// Press <Enter>

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 7.	When prompted 'Want to DISABLE Scheduled Options, Menu Options, and Protocols? NO// Press <Enter>
texts.text: 7.	When prompted 'Want to DISABLE Scheduled Options, Menu Options, and Protocols? NO// Press <Enter>

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 8.	If prompted 'Delay Install (Minutes): (0-60): 0//', press <Enter>.
texts.text: 8.	If prompted 'Delay Install (Minutes): (0-60): 0//', press <Enter>.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 9.	If prompted 'Enter the Device you want to print the Install  messages. You can queue the install by enter a 'Q' at the device prompt. Enter a '^' to abort the install. DEVICE: HOME//   HOME (CRT)', press <Enter>.
texts.text: 9.	If prompted 'Enter the Device you want to print the Install  messages. You can queue the install by enter a 'Q' at the device prompt. Enter a '^' to abort the install. DEVICE: HOME//   HOME (CRT)', press <Enter>.

texts.text: 6.3 Post-Installation Instructions

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The mail group: ADVERSE_ALLERGY_WARNING was added with this patch.
texts.text: The mail group: ADVERSE_ALLERGY_WARNING was added with this patch.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Appropriate persons will need to be added to this mail group that should  receive the email when the VA Drug Class field is empty.
texts.text: Appropriate persons will need to be added to this mail group that should  receive the email when the VA Drug Class field is empty.
